revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:日本国政府, en:Government of Japan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/72514905 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 政府テンプレ == 政府テンプレを張り付ける場合はその表記に付き、出典を付けるようお願いします（[[Wikipedia:出典を明記する]]）。--[[利用者:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2014年7月9日 (水) 13:10 (UTC) *再び政府テンプレが張り付けられましたが、1946年に「日本国政府」が新規に作られ、「大日本帝国政府」は前政府であるという件について出典が付けられておりません。なぜか「大日本帝国政府」には「明治維新」がリンク先に張り付けられてましたが、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集でしょうか？これは独自研究だということは「大日本帝国」の項目のノートの議論でも出ており、建国1868年、滅亡1947年に書き換えた編集が差し戻された経緯もございます。--[[利用者:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年3月29日 (金) 08:22 (UTC) 政府テンプレートはブリタニカ国際大百科事典『王政復古』を根拠として作成いたしましたが、どのあたりが独自研究に当たるのでしょう。ご教示いただけませんか？ [[利用者:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:04 (UTC) 『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」を政府成立年の根拠とし、前政府の記述を削除すれば、テンプレートは独自研究に当たらないでしょうか？ [[利用者:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:06 (UTC) :そもそもこんなテンプレは不要。Omaemona1982さんが指摘されているように、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集は独自研究そのものでしょ。そんなマイナーな歴史観をWikipediaで披露しないでください。日本国憲法は大日本帝国憲法の改正で成立したものであり、憲法改正のたびに政府が廃止されたり新規に出来たりするわけじゃありません。--[[利用者:ササニシキ|ササニシキ]]（[[利用者‐会話:ササニシキ|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 14:24 (UTC) :{{コメント}} おおもとの[[Template:政府]]は当記事だけでなく、[[特別:リンク元/Template:政府|結構あちこち]]で使われているようです。とりわけ「前政府」と「後政府」の項は盛大に独自研究が入ってますね。ところで、このテンプレートと似ている、[[Template‐ノート:政府#より汎用的なテンプレートの提案]]でご紹介した英語版の事例もあります。もしよろしければ、あちらでもご意見をお聞かせください。--[[利用者:Doraemonplus|Doraemonplus]]（[[利用者‐会話:Doraemonplus|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 15:56 (UTC) :他の項目で使っているからと本稿で使わなければならない理由にはならないと思います。私も本稿には政府テンプレは不要だと思います。図書館の事典やコトバンクの検索でざっと調べた限りでは政府の創始年の記述らしきものは『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」の項目に「日本の近代政府は、旧来の太政官や参議の制度を廃した1885年の内閣制に始まる。」という表記が出てくるぐらいでした。百科事典は三次資料で出典能力がないわけではありませんが、著者の記名がないものは編集員が書いたものなので注意が必要なものもあるとされていることもあり（[[Wikipedia:信頼できる情報源]]）、断定・特定的になってしまうテンプレでこれのみを出典に政府成立年を断定的に記述するのもいかがなものかと思います。そもそもテンプレはわかりやすくするために置かれる物であって長い備考や諸説をずらずら載せるものではありませんし。日本政府やイギリス政府のような成立年を特定することが困難な政府にまでテンプレで無理やり規定する必要はないと考えます。--[[利用者:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 16:24 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1337761585 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{GA|18:26, 6 September 2015 (UTC)|page=1|topic=Politics and government|oldid=712510332}} {{Talk header}} {{DYK talk|2 October|2015|entry= ... that the '''[[government of Japan]]''' is a [[constitutional monarchy]] whereby the power of the [[Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] is limited and is relegated primarily to ceremonial duties?|nompage=Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=GA|vital=yes|1= {{WikiProject Politics|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|law=yes|importance=Top}} }} ==Project Assessment== Beautifully structured, but a bit short on content. Sure, most of the content can be found in separate articles on the Prime Minister, Diet, etc etc, but I think we really ought to have a bit more here nevertheless. Summary paragraphs. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 23:29, 29 December 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Introduction == The introduction here is really short, and instead of introducing the topic, it gives a contrast to something else. I might fix it when I have time.[[User:Rhinocerous Ranger|Rhinocerous Ranger]] ([[User talk:Rhinocerous Ranger|talk]]) 17:01, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :I agree. In particular, I have never understood why it begins with "Japan no longer officially has the traditional federal system," I mean, does Japan have traditional federal system ''unofficially''? Very strange first sentence. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] ([[User talk:TakuyaMurata|talk]]) 22:51, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Important notice == The [[Outline of Japan#Government and politics of Japan|government section of the "Outline of Japan"]] needs to be checked, corrected, and completed -- ''especially the subsections for the government branches''. When the country outlines were created, temporary data (that matched most of the countries but not all) was used to speed up the process. Those countries for which the temporary data does not match must be replaced with the correct information. Please check that this country's outline is not in error. If you have any questions or comments, please contact [[User talk:The Transhumanist|The Transhumanist]] . Thank you. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Why did Wikiepeida list insulting information of Japan? == The Government of Japan article earlier had written in Korean "독도만세일본 시발놈들아 이 원숭이새끼들아" at the start of the article. Please explain why this was listed. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/1.251.19.69|1.251.19.69]] ([[User talk:1.251.19.69|talk]]) 05:14, 9 November 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Position of the emperor == There seems to be some confusion regarding the position of the emperor. He is clearly the head of state. Emperors, Kaisers, Kings, Czars, Presidents, etc. are all by definition heads of states - if the respective person abdicates, loses his position or the political system changes that person is no longer called emperor or a kaiser, president, king (because those titles denote a political position at the top of a state structure) but is instead called "royal highness" (like the abdicated king Edward), just "Mister/Herr" Otto Habsburg (in the case of the son of the abdicated Emperor of Austria) or "former president". A lot of constitutions do not explicitly name a head of state; the German constitution for example does not explicitly state that the federal president is the head of state, does that mean that Germany does not have a head of state? Of course not, the title "Federal President" in the context of the constitution clearly means "head of state" and denotes a political position, just like the title "Emperor". The constitution of Denmark also does not explicitly call the King "head of state", nonetheless it is clear that a "King", by definition, is a head of state. The usage of the symbol-metaphor is also not that uncommon as both the King of Spain and the King of Cambodia are described as symbols of the state or the nation in their respective constitutions. If the emperor really is just the "Symbol" and nothing else, then what is Japan? A symbolocracy? Of course not, it's a constitutional monarchy and the emperor is the ceremonial (i.e. non-executive) head of state. As I do not want to start an edit-war I will refrain from changing the article but it is very misleading and also inconsistent to state that the emperor is not the head of state. The opening section regarding the emperor is also not only poorly written but also not properly referenced and does not cite any scientific sources, leading me to the conclusion that the editor is just stating his personal opinion. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/141.48.179.241|141.48.179.241]] ([[User talk:141.48.179.241|talk]]) 15:26, 10 January 2015 (UTC) :I agree, it makes sense. However, regarding the last paragraph, I think there is some confusion on what a Constitution is. A Constitution defines the principles on how the state is being governed. A Constitution constitutes(defines) on how the state is being run, and not simply describe roughly how it runs. In this case, the Constitution drafted by the previous government defined the new role of the Emperor, and not simply describe how he should be. The Constitution requires the Emperor to act in such a manner, and he must do so. If he does not, it would be a violation of the Constitution. If the Constitution just simply describes the Emperor on how he should be, then isn't he able to just ignore it and say that it describes him wrongly. Agree? --[[User:TheInfernoX|TheInfernoX]] ([[User talk:TheInfernoX|talk]]) 15:00, 8 February 2015 (UTC) Yes, I agree. Poor choice of words, english is not my first language. The last paragraph looks completely different now; earlier versions were very convoluted and stated that the emperor is not the head of state (the reference was some japantimes.com article ...). [[Special:Contributions/86.56.43.81|86.56.43.81]] ([[User talk:86.56.43.81|talk]]) 20:16, 16 February 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == URLs of former government ministries == If you want to look up URLs of former government ministries on the wayback machine, they are listed here: http://web.archive.org/web/19980125145714/http://www.jinjapan.org/jd/org/002001002.html#002001002026 - http://www.webcitation.org/6WiHjhitQ [[User:WhisperToMe|WhisperToMe]] ([[User talk:WhisperToMe|talk]]) 22:09, 1 March 2015 (UTC) {{Talk:Government of Japan/GA1}} {{Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == A rogue article == "Before The Meiji Restoration, Japan was ruled by the government of a successive military shōgun." It looks as if a bumbling editor has inserted the word "a" in this sentence, not understanding that "shōgun" here is being used as a plural. If I interpret this correctly, the "a" ought to be removed. [[Special:Contributions/71.245.188.249|71.245.188.249]] ([[User talk:71.245.188.249|talk]]) 07:19, 21 September 2023 (UTC) :I think there are other problems with how ''shōgun'' is rendered in this article, but for the time being I've corrected this. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:10, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Could someone with knowledge correct this? == Under "History," there's an odd paratactic construction: :"Taiseihokan"（大政奉還）The return of political power to the Emperor (to the Imperial Court) in 1868 meant the resignation of Shōgun [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]], agreeing to "be the instrument for carrying out" the Emperor's orders. The capitalized "The" before "return" indicates the start of a sentence, so how does "Taiseihokan"（大政奉還) relate to the syntax of that following sentence? I don't know Japanese at all; Google Translate tells me that ''Taiseihokan'' means "system storage"? So could someone who understands the topic and the intention here be able to correct this? [[User:Cynwolfe|Cynwolfe]] ([[User talk:Cynwolfe|talk]]) 16:30, 15 January 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Inconsistent rendering of ''shōgun'' == The word ''shōgun'' is rendered as both ''shōgun'' and Shōgun (no italics). One rendering should be used consistently throughout the article. The article for [[shogun]] does not use italics, a macron above the o, or capitalisation because the word is pretty well known t English speaking audiences. I think this would be fine. But if the macron should be kept then every instance should be in italics because it's a romanisation. And there's no reason to capitalise it. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:14, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Change how ''kazoku'' is rendered == I think it may be slightly confusing for people with some knowledge of Japanese but not much knowledge of the Japanese feudal system that there is no kanji given for this word. The most common word pronounced ''kazoku'' is 家族, but the word being used in this article is 華族 and they mean different things. I suggest that this be added for clarity. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:16, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: I guess I am immediately moving more things than I thought with this template. But hope this goes well. My bot uses a different named header template so we are using it
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/72514905 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 政府テンプレ == 政府テンプレを張り付ける場合はその表記に付き、出典を付けるようお願いします（[[Wikipedia:出典を明記する]]）。--[[利用者:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2014年7月9日 (水) 13:10 (UTC) *再び政府テンプレが張り付けられましたが、1946年に「日本国政府」が新規に作られ、「大日本帝国政府」は前政府であるという件について出典が付けられておりません。なぜか「大日本帝国政府」には「明治維新」がリンク先に張り付けられてましたが、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集でしょうか？これは独自研究だということは「大日本帝国」の項目のノートの議論でも出ており、建国1868年、滅亡1947年に書き換えた編集が差し戻された経緯もございます。--[[利用者:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年3月29日 (金) 08:22 (UTC) 政府テンプレートはブリタニカ国際大百科事典『王政復古』を根拠として作成いたしましたが、どのあたりが独自研究に当たるのでしょう。ご教示いただけませんか？ [[利用者:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:04 (UTC) 『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」を政府成立年の根拠とし、前政府の記述を削除すれば、テンプレートは独自研究に当たらないでしょうか？ [[利用者:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:06 (UTC) :そもそもこんなテンプレは不要。Omaemona1982さんが指摘されているように、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集は独自研究そのものでしょ。そんなマイナーな歴史観をWikipediaで披露しないでください。日本国憲法は大日本帝国憲法の改正で成立したものであり、憲法改正のたびに政府が廃止されたり新規に出来たりするわけじゃありません。--[[利用者:ササニシキ|ササニシキ]]（[[利用者‐会話:ササニシキ|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 14:24 (UTC) :{{コメント}} おおもとの[[Template:政府]]は当記事だけでなく、[[特別:リンク元/Template:政府|結構あちこち]]で使われているようです。とりわけ「前政府」と「後政府」の項は盛大に独自研究が入ってますね。ところで、このテンプレートと似ている、[[Template‐ノート:政府#より汎用的なテンプレートの提案]]でご紹介した英語版の事例もあります。もしよろしければ、あちらでもご意見をお聞かせください。--[[利用者:Doraemonplus|Doraemonplus]]（[[利用者‐会話:Doraemonplus|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 15:56 (UTC) :他の項目で使っているからと本稿で使わなければならない理由にはならないと思います。私も本稿には政府テンプレは不要だと思います。図書館の事典やコトバンクの検索でざっと調べた限りでは政府の創始年の記述らしきものは『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」の項目に「日本の近代政府は、旧来の太政官や参議の制度を廃した1885年の内閣制に始まる。」という表記が出てくるぐらいでした。百科事典は三次資料で出典能力がないわけではありませんが、著者の記名がないものは編集員が書いたものなので注意が必要なものもあるとされていることもあり（[[Wikipedia:信頼できる情報源]]）、断定・特定的になってしまうテンプレでこれのみを出典に政府成立年を断定的に記述するのもいかがなものかと思います。そもそもテンプレはわかりやすくするために置かれる物であって長い備考や諸説をずらずら載せるものではありませんし。日本政府やイギリス政府のような成立年を特定することが困難な政府にまでテンプレで無理やり規定する必要はないと考えます。--[[利用者:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 16:24 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1337761585 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{GA|18:26, 6 September 2015 (UTC)|page=1|topic=Politics and government|oldid=712510332}} {{Talk page header}} {{DYK talk|2 October|2015|entry= ... that the '''[[government of Japan]]''' is a [[constitutional monarchy]] whereby the power of the [[Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] is limited and is relegated primarily to ceremonial duties?|nompage=Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=GA|vital=yes|1= {{WikiProject Politics|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|law=yes|importance=Top}} }} ==Project Assessment== Beautifully structured, but a bit short on content. Sure, most of the content can be found in separate articles on the Prime Minister, Diet, etc etc, but I think we really ought to have a bit more here nevertheless. Summary paragraphs. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 23:29, 29 December 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Introduction == The introduction here is really short, and instead of introducing the topic, it gives a contrast to something else. I might fix it when I have time.[[User:Rhinocerous Ranger|Rhinocerous Ranger]] ([[User talk:Rhinocerous Ranger|talk]]) 17:01, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :I agree. In particular, I have never understood why it begins with "Japan no longer officially has the traditional federal system," I mean, does Japan have traditional federal system ''unofficially''? Very strange first sentence. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] ([[User talk:TakuyaMurata|talk]]) 22:51, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Important notice == The [[Outline of Japan#Government and politics of Japan|government section of the "Outline of Japan"]] needs to be checked, corrected, and completed -- ''especially the subsections for the government branches''. When the country outlines were created, temporary data (that matched most of the countries but not all) was used to speed up the process. Those countries for which the temporary data does not match must be replaced with the correct information. Please check that this country's outline is not in error. If you have any questions or comments, please contact [[User talk:The Transhumanist|The Transhumanist]] . Thank you. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Why did Wikiepeida list insulting information of Japan? == The Government of Japan article earlier had written in Korean "독도만세일본 시발놈들아 이 원숭이새끼들아" at the start of the article. Please explain why this was listed. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/1.251.19.69|1.251.19.69]] ([[User talk:1.251.19.69|talk]]) 05:14, 9 November 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Position of the emperor == There seems to be some confusion regarding the position of the emperor. He is clearly the head of state. Emperors, Kaisers, Kings, Czars, Presidents, etc. are all by definition heads of states - if the respective person abdicates, loses his position or the political system changes that person is no longer called emperor or a kaiser, president, king (because those titles denote a political position at the top of a state structure) but is instead called "royal highness" (like the abdicated king Edward), just "Mister/Herr" Otto Habsburg (in the case of the son of the abdicated Emperor of Austria) or "former president". A lot of constitutions do not explicitly name a head of state; the German constitution for example does not explicitly state that the federal president is the head of state, does that mean that Germany does not have a head of state? Of course not, the title "Federal President" in the context of the constitution clearly means "head of state" and denotes a political position, just like the title "Emperor". The constitution of Denmark also does not explicitly call the King "head of state", nonetheless it is clear that a "King", by definition, is a head of state. The usage of the symbol-metaphor is also not that uncommon as both the King of Spain and the King of Cambodia are described as symbols of the state or the nation in their respective constitutions. If the emperor really is just the "Symbol" and nothing else, then what is Japan? A symbolocracy? Of course not, it's a constitutional monarchy and the emperor is the ceremonial (i.e. non-executive) head of state. As I do not want to start an edit-war I will refrain from changing the article but it is very misleading and also inconsistent to state that the emperor is not the head of state. The opening section regarding the emperor is also not only poorly written but also not properly referenced and does not cite any scientific sources, leading me to the conclusion that the editor is just stating his personal opinion. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/141.48.179.241|141.48.179.241]] ([[User talk:141.48.179.241|talk]]) 15:26, 10 January 2015 (UTC) :I agree, it makes sense. However, regarding the last paragraph, I think there is some confusion on what a Constitution is. A Constitution defines the principles on how the state is being governed. A Constitution constitutes(defines) on how the state is being run, and not simply describe roughly how it runs. In this case, the Constitution drafted by the previous government defined the new role of the Emperor, and not simply describe how he should be. The Constitution requires the Emperor to act in such a manner, and he must do so. If he does not, it would be a violation of the Constitution. If the Constitution just simply describes the Emperor on how he should be, then isn't he able to just ignore it and say that it describes him wrongly. Agree? --[[User:TheInfernoX|TheInfernoX]] ([[User talk:TheInfernoX|talk]]) 15:00, 8 February 2015 (UTC) Yes, I agree. Poor choice of words, english is not my first language. The last paragraph looks completely different now; earlier versions were very convoluted and stated that the emperor is not the head of state (the reference was some japantimes.com article ...). [[Special:Contributions/86.56.43.81|86.56.43.81]] ([[User talk:86.56.43.81|talk]]) 20:16, 16 February 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == URLs of former government ministries == If you want to look up URLs of former government ministries on the wayback machine, they are listed here: http://web.archive.org/web/19980125145714/http://www.jinjapan.org/jd/org/002001002.html#002001002026 - http://www.webcitation.org/6WiHjhitQ [[User:WhisperToMe|WhisperToMe]] ([[User talk:WhisperToMe|talk]]) 22:09, 1 March 2015 (UTC) {{Talk:Government of Japan/GA1}} {{Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == A rogue article == "Before The Meiji Restoration, Japan was ruled by the government of a successive military shōgun." It looks as if a bumbling editor has inserted the word "a" in this sentence, not understanding that "shōgun" here is being used as a plural. If I interpret this correctly, the "a" ought to be removed. [[Special:Contributions/71.245.188.249|71.245.188.249]] ([[User talk:71.245.188.249|talk]]) 07:19, 21 September 2023 (UTC) :I think there are other problems with how ''shōgun'' is rendered in this article, but for the time being I've corrected this. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:10, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Could someone with knowledge correct this? == Under "History," there's an odd paratactic construction: :"Taiseihokan"（大政奉還）The return of political power to the Emperor (to the Imperial Court) in 1868 meant the resignation of Shōgun [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]], agreeing to "be the instrument for carrying out" the Emperor's orders. The capitalized "The" before "return" indicates the start of a sentence, so how does "Taiseihokan"（大政奉還) relate to the syntax of that following sentence? I don't know Japanese at all; Google Translate tells me that ''Taiseihokan'' means "system storage"? So could someone who understands the topic and the intention here be able to correct this? [[User:Cynwolfe|Cynwolfe]] ([[User talk:Cynwolfe|talk]]) 16:30, 15 January 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Inconsistent rendering of ''shōgun'' == The word ''shōgun'' is rendered as both ''shōgun'' and Shōgun (no italics). One rendering should be used consistently throughout the article. The article for [[shogun]] does not use italics, a macron above the o, or capitalisation because the word is pretty well known t English speaking audiences. I think this would be fine. But if the macron should be kept then every instance should be in italics because it's a romanisation. And there's no reason to capitalise it. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:14, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Change how ''kazoku'' is rendered == I think it may be slightly confusing for people with some knowledge of Japanese but not much knowledge of the Japanese feudal system that there is no kanji given for this word. The most common word pronounced ''kazoku'' is 家族, but the word being used in this article is 華族 and they mean different things. I suggest that this be added for clarity. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:16, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/72514905 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 政府テンプレ == 政府テンプレを張り付ける場合はその表記に付き、出典を付けるようお願いします（[[Wikipedia:出典を明記する]]）。--[[User:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2014年7月9日 (水) 13:10 (UTC) *再び政府テンプレが張り付けられましたが、1946年に「日本国政府」が新規に作られ、「大日本帝国政府」は前政府であるという件について出典が付けられておりません。なぜか「大日本帝国政府」には「明治維新」がリンク先に張り付けられてましたが、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集でしょうか？これは独自研究だということは「大日本帝国」の項目のノートの議論でも出ており、建国1868年、滅亡1947年に書き換えた編集が差し戻された経緯もございます。--[[User:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年3月29日 (金) 08:22 (UTC) 政府テンプレートはブリタニカ国際大百科事典『王政復古』を根拠として作成いたしましたが、どのあたりが独自研究に当たるのでしょう。ご教示いただけませんか？ [[User:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:04 (UTC) 『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」を政府成立年の根拠とし、前政府の記述を削除すれば、テンプレートは独自研究に当たらないでしょうか？ [[User:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:06 (UTC) :そもそもこんなテンプレは不要。Omaemona1982さんが指摘されているように、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集は独自研究そのものでしょ。そんなマイナーな歴史観をWikipediaで披露しないでください。日本国憲法は大日本帝国憲法の改正で成立したものであり、憲法改正のたびに政府が廃止されたり新規に出来たりするわけじゃありません。--[[User:ササニシキ|ササニシキ]]（[[利用者‐会話:ササニシキ|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 14:24 (UTC) :{{コメント}} おおもとの[[Template:政府]]は当記事だけでなく、[[特別:リンク元/Template:政府|結構あちこち]]で使われているようです。とりわけ「前政府」と「後政府」の項は盛大に独自研究が入ってますね。ところで、このテンプレートと似ている、[[Template‐ノート:政府#より汎用的なテンプレートの提案]]でご紹介した英語版の事例もあります。もしよろしければ、あちらでもご意見をお聞かせください。--[[User:Doraemonplus|Doraemonplus]]（[[利用者‐会話:Doraemonplus|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 15:56 (UTC) :他の項目で使っているからと本稿で使わなければならない理由にはならないと思います。私も本稿には政府テンプレは不要だと思います。図書館の事典やコトバンクの検索でざっと調べた限りでは政府の創始年の記述らしきものは『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」の項目に「日本の近代政府は、旧来の太政官や参議の制度を廃した1885年の内閣制に始まる。」という表記が出てくるぐらいでした。百科事典は三次資料で出典能力がないわけではありませんが、著者の記名がないものは編集員が書いたものなので注意が必要なものもあるとされていることもあり（[[Wikipedia:信頼できる情報源]]）、断定・特定的になってしまうテンプレでこれのみを出典に政府成立年を断定的に記述するのもいかがなものかと思います。そもそもテンプレはわかりやすくするために置かれる物であって長い備考や諸説をずらずら載せるものではありませんし。日本政府やイギリス政府のような成立年を特定することが困難な政府にまでテンプレで無理やり規定する必要はないと考えます。--[[User:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 16:24 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1337761585 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{GA|18:26, 6 September 2015 (UTC)|page=1|topic=Politics and government|oldid=712510332}} {{Talk page header}} {{DYK talk|2 October|2015|entry= ... that the '''[[government of Japan]]''' is a [[constitutional monarchy]] whereby the power of the [[Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] is limited and is relegated primarily to ceremonial duties?|nompage=Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=GA|vital=yes|1= {{WikiProject Politics|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|law=yes|importance=Top}} }} ==Project Assessment== Beautifully structured, but a bit short on content. Sure, most of the content can be found in separate articles on the Prime Minister, Diet, etc etc, but I think we really ought to have a bit more here nevertheless. Summary paragraphs. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 23:29, 29 December 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Introduction == The introduction here is really short, and instead of introducing the topic, it gives a contrast to something else. I might fix it when I have time.[[User:Rhinocerous Ranger|Rhinocerous Ranger]] ([[User talk:Rhinocerous Ranger|talk]]) 17:01, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :I agree. In particular, I have never understood why it begins with "Japan no longer officially has the traditional federal system," I mean, does Japan have traditional federal system ''unofficially''? Very strange first sentence. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] ([[User talk:TakuyaMurata|talk]]) 22:51, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Important notice == The [[Outline of Japan#Government and politics of Japan|government section of the "Outline of Japan"]] needs to be checked, corrected, and completed -- ''especially the subsections for the government branches''. When the country outlines were created, temporary data (that matched most of the countries but not all) was used to speed up the process. Those countries for which the temporary data does not match must be replaced with the correct information. Please check that this country's outline is not in error. If you have any questions or comments, please contact [[User talk:The Transhumanist|The Transhumanist]] . Thank you. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Why did Wikiepeida list insulting information of Japan? == The Government of Japan article earlier had written in Korean "독도만세일본 시발놈들아 이 원숭이새끼들아" at the start of the article. Please explain why this was listed. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/1.251.19.69|1.251.19.69]] ([[User talk:1.251.19.69|talk]]) 05:14, 9 November 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Position of the emperor == There seems to be some confusion regarding the position of the emperor. He is clearly the head of state. Emperors, Kaisers, Kings, Czars, Presidents, etc. are all by definition heads of states - if the respective person abdicates, loses his position or the political system changes that person is no longer called emperor or a kaiser, president, king (because those titles denote a political position at the top of a state structure) but is instead called "royal highness" (like the abdicated king Edward), just "Mister/Herr" Otto Habsburg (in the case of the son of the abdicated Emperor of Austria) or "former president". A lot of constitutions do not explicitly name a head of state; the German constitution for example does not explicitly state that the federal president is the head of state, does that mean that Germany does not have a head of state? Of course not, the title "Federal President" in the context of the constitution clearly means "head of state" and denotes a political position, just like the title "Emperor". The constitution of Denmark also does not explicitly call the King "head of state", nonetheless it is clear that a "King", by definition, is a head of state. The usage of the symbol-metaphor is also not that uncommon as both the King of Spain and the King of Cambodia are described as symbols of the state or the nation in their respective constitutions. If the emperor really is just the "Symbol" and nothing else, then what is Japan? A symbolocracy? Of course not, it's a constitutional monarchy and the emperor is the ceremonial (i.e. non-executive) head of state. As I do not want to start an edit-war I will refrain from changing the article but it is very misleading and also inconsistent to state that the emperor is not the head of state. The opening section regarding the emperor is also not only poorly written but also not properly referenced and does not cite any scientific sources, leading me to the conclusion that the editor is just stating his personal opinion. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/141.48.179.241|141.48.179.241]] ([[User talk:141.48.179.241|talk]]) 15:26, 10 January 2015 (UTC) :I agree, it makes sense. However, regarding the last paragraph, I think there is some confusion on what a Constitution is. A Constitution defines the principles on how the state is being governed. A Constitution constitutes(defines) on how the state is being run, and not simply describe roughly how it runs. In this case, the Constitution drafted by the previous government defined the new role of the Emperor, and not simply describe how he should be. The Constitution requires the Emperor to act in such a manner, and he must do so. If he does not, it would be a violation of the Constitution. If the Constitution just simply describes the Emperor on how he should be, then isn't he able to just ignore it and say that it describes him wrongly. Agree? --[[User:TheInfernoX|TheInfernoX]] ([[User talk:TheInfernoX|talk]]) 15:00, 8 February 2015 (UTC) Yes, I agree. Poor choice of words, english is not my first language. The last paragraph looks completely different now; earlier versions were very convoluted and stated that the emperor is not the head of state (the reference was some japantimes.com article ...). [[Special:Contributions/86.56.43.81|86.56.43.81]] ([[User talk:86.56.43.81|talk]]) 20:16, 16 February 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == URLs of former government ministries == If you want to look up URLs of former government ministries on the wayback machine, they are listed here: http://web.archive.org/web/19980125145714/http://www.jinjapan.org/jd/org/002001002.html#002001002026 - http://www.webcitation.org/6WiHjhitQ [[User:WhisperToMe|WhisperToMe]] ([[User talk:WhisperToMe|talk]]) 22:09, 1 March 2015 (UTC) {{Talk:Government of Japan/GA1}} {{Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == A rogue article == "Before The Meiji Restoration, Japan was ruled by the government of a successive military shōgun." It looks as if a bumbling editor has inserted the word "a" in this sentence, not understanding that "shōgun" here is being used as a plural. If I interpret this correctly, the "a" ought to be removed. [[Special:Contributions/71.245.188.249|71.245.188.249]] ([[User talk:71.245.188.249|talk]]) 07:19, 21 September 2023 (UTC) :I think there are other problems with how ''shōgun'' is rendered in this article, but for the time being I've corrected this. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:10, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Could someone with knowledge correct this? == Under "History," there's an odd paratactic construction: :"Taiseihokan"（大政奉還）The return of political power to the Emperor (to the Imperial Court) in 1868 meant the resignation of Shōgun [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]], agreeing to "be the instrument for carrying out" the Emperor's orders. The capitalized "The" before "return" indicates the start of a sentence, so how does "Taiseihokan"（大政奉還) relate to the syntax of that following sentence? I don't know Japanese at all; Google Translate tells me that ''Taiseihokan'' means "system storage"? So could someone who understands the topic and the intention here be able to correct this? [[User:Cynwolfe|Cynwolfe]] ([[User talk:Cynwolfe|talk]]) 16:30, 15 January 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Inconsistent rendering of ''shōgun'' == The word ''shōgun'' is rendered as both ''shōgun'' and Shōgun (no italics). One rendering should be used consistently throughout the article. The article for [[shogun]] does not use italics, a macron above the o, or capitalisation because the word is pretty well known t English speaking audiences. I think this would be fine. But if the macron should be kept then every instance should be in italics because it's a romanisation. And there's no reason to capitalise it. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:14, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Change how ''kazoku'' is rendered == I think it may be slightly confusing for people with some knowledge of Japanese but not much knowledge of the Japanese feudal system that there is no kanji given for this word. The most common word pronounced ''kazoku'' is 家族, but the word being used in this article is 華族 and they mean different things. I suggest that this be added for clarity. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:16, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:14.192.212.242]], [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Government_of_Japan here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/72514905 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 政府テンプレ == 政府テンプレを張り付ける場合はその表記に付き、出典を付けるようお願いします（[[Wikipedia:出典を明記する]]）。--[[User:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2014年7月9日 (水) 13:10 (UTC) *再び政府テンプレが張り付けられましたが、1946年に「日本国政府」が新規に作られ、「大日本帝国政府」は前政府であるという件について出典が付けられておりません。なぜか「大日本帝国政府」には「明治維新」がリンク先に張り付けられてましたが、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集でしょうか？これは独自研究だということは「大日本帝国」の項目のノートの議論でも出ており、建国1868年、滅亡1947年に書き換えた編集が差し戻された経緯もございます。--[[User:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年3月29日 (金) 08:22 (UTC) 政府テンプレートはブリタニカ国際大百科事典『王政復古』を根拠として作成いたしましたが、どのあたりが独自研究に当たるのでしょう。ご教示いただけませんか？ [[User:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:04 (UTC) 『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」を政府成立年の根拠とし、前政府の記述を削除すれば、テンプレートは独自研究に当たらないでしょうか？ [[User:Shuricastle|Shuricastle]]（[[利用者‐会話:Shuricastle|会話]]） 2019年4月23日 (火) 07:06 (UTC) :そもそもこんなテンプレは不要。Omaemona1982さんが指摘されているように、「大日本帝国」が1868年に作られ、1946年に終わったという立場からの編集は独自研究そのものでしょ。そんなマイナーな歴史観をWikipediaで披露しないでください。日本国憲法は大日本帝国憲法の改正で成立したものであり、憲法改正のたびに政府が廃止されたり新規に出来たりするわけじゃありません。--[[User:ササニシキ|ササニシキ]]（[[利用者‐会話:ササニシキ|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 14:24 (UTC) :{{コメント}} おおもとの[[Template:政府]]は当記事だけでなく、[[特別:リンク元/Template:政府|結構あちこち]]で使われているようです。とりわけ「前政府」と「後政府」の項は盛大に独自研究が入ってますね。ところで、このテンプレートと似ている、[[Template‐ノート:政府#より汎用的なテンプレートの提案]]でご紹介した英語版の事例もあります。もしよろしければ、あちらでもご意見をお聞かせください。--[[User:Doraemonplus|Doraemonplus]]（[[利用者‐会話:Doraemonplus|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 15:56 (UTC) :他の項目で使っているからと本稿で使わなければならない理由にはならないと思います。私も本稿には政府テンプレは不要だと思います。図書館の事典やコトバンクの検索でざっと調べた限りでは政府の創始年の記述らしきものは『百科事典マイペディア』（平凡社）「政府」の項目に「日本の近代政府は、旧来の太政官や参議の制度を廃した1885年の内閣制に始まる。」という表記が出てくるぐらいでした。百科事典は三次資料で出典能力がないわけではありませんが、著者の記名がないものは編集員が書いたものなので注意が必要なものもあるとされていることもあり（[[Wikipedia:信頼できる情報源]]）、断定・特定的になってしまうテンプレでこれのみを出典に政府成立年を断定的に記述するのもいかがなものかと思います。そもそもテンプレはわかりやすくするために置かれる物であって長い備考や諸説をずらずら載せるものではありませんし。日本政府やイギリス政府のような成立年を特定することが困難な政府にまでテンプレで無理やり規定する必要はないと考えます。--[[User:Omaemona1982|Omaemona1982]]（[[利用者‐会話:Omaemona1982|会話]]） 2019年4月25日 (木) 16:24 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1337761585 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{GA|18:26, 6 September 2015 (UTC)|page=1|topic=Politics and government|oldid=712510332}} {{Talk page header}} {{DYK talk|2 October|2015|entry= ... that the '''[[government of Japan]]''' is a [[constitutional monarchy]] whereby the power of the [[Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] is limited and is relegated primarily to ceremonial duties?|nompage=Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=GA|vital=yes|1= {{WikiProject Politics|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|law=yes|importance=Top}} }} ==Project Assessment== Beautifully structured, but a bit short on content. Sure, most of the content can be found in separate articles on the Prime Minister, Diet, etc etc, but I think we really ought to have a bit more here nevertheless. Summary paragraphs. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 23:29, 29 December 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Introduction == The introduction here is really short, and instead of introducing the topic, it gives a contrast to something else. I might fix it when I have time.[[User:Rhinocerous Ranger|Rhinocerous Ranger]] ([[User talk:Rhinocerous Ranger|talk]]) 17:01, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :I agree. In particular, I have never understood why it begins with "Japan no longer officially has the traditional federal system," I mean, does Japan have traditional federal system ''unofficially''? Very strange first sentence. -- [[User:TakuyaMurata|Taku]] ([[User talk:TakuyaMurata|talk]]) 22:51, 7 May 2008 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Important notice == The [[Outline of Japan#Government and politics of Japan|government section of the "Outline of Japan"]] needs to be checked, corrected, and completed -- ''especially the subsections for the government branches''. When the country outlines were created, temporary data (that matched most of the countries but not all) was used to speed up the process. Those countries for which the temporary data does not match must be replaced with the correct information. Please check that this country's outline is not in error. If you have any questions or comments, please contact [[User talk:The Transhumanist|The Transhumanist]] . Thank you. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Why did Wikiepeida list insulting information of Japan? == The Government of Japan article earlier had written in Korean "독도만세일본 시발놈들아 이 원숭이새끼들아" at the start of the article. Please explain why this was listed. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/1.251.19.69|1.251.19.69]] ([[User talk:1.251.19.69|talk]]) 05:14, 9 November 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Position of the emperor == There seems to be some confusion regarding the position of the emperor. He is clearly the head of state. Emperors, Kaisers, Kings, Czars, Presidents, etc. are all by definition heads of states - if the respective person abdicates, loses his position or the political system changes that person is no longer called emperor or a kaiser, president, king (because those titles denote a political position at the top of a state structure) but is instead called "royal highness" (like the abdicated king Edward), just "Mister/Herr" Otto Habsburg (in the case of the son of the abdicated Emperor of Austria) or "former president". A lot of constitutions do not explicitly name a head of state; the German constitution for example does not explicitly state that the federal president is the head of state, does that mean that Germany does not have a head of state? Of course not, the title "Federal President" in the context of the constitution clearly means "head of state" and denotes a political position, just like the title "Emperor". The constitution of Denmark also does not explicitly call the King "head of state", nonetheless it is clear that a "King", by definition, is a head of state. The usage of the symbol-metaphor is also not that uncommon as both the King of Spain and the King of Cambodia are described as symbols of the state or the nation in their respective constitutions. If the emperor really is just the "Symbol" and nothing else, then what is Japan? A symbolocracy? Of course not, it's a constitutional monarchy and the emperor is the ceremonial (i.e. non-executive) head of state. As I do not want to start an edit-war I will refrain from changing the article but it is very misleading and also inconsistent to state that the emperor is not the head of state. The opening section regarding the emperor is also not only poorly written but also not properly referenced and does not cite any scientific sources, leading me to the conclusion that the editor is just stating his personal opinion. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/141.48.179.241|141.48.179.241]] ([[User talk:141.48.179.241|talk]]) 15:26, 10 January 2015 (UTC) :I agree, it makes sense. However, regarding the last paragraph, I think there is some confusion on what a Constitution is. A Constitution defines the principles on how the state is being governed. A Constitution constitutes(defines) on how the state is being run, and not simply describe roughly how it runs. In this case, the Constitution drafted by the previous government defined the new role of the Emperor, and not simply describe how he should be. The Constitution requires the Emperor to act in such a manner, and he must do so. If he does not, it would be a violation of the Constitution. If the Constitution just simply describes the Emperor on how he should be, then isn't he able to just ignore it and say that it describes him wrongly. Agree? --[[User:TheInfernoX|TheInfernoX]] ([[User talk:TheInfernoX|talk]]) 15:00, 8 February 2015 (UTC) Yes, I agree. Poor choice of words, english is not my first language. The last paragraph looks completely different now; earlier versions were very convoluted and stated that the emperor is not the head of state (the reference was some japantimes.com article ...). [[Special:Contributions/86.56.43.81|86.56.43.81]] ([[User talk:86.56.43.81|talk]]) 20:16, 16 February 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == URLs of former government ministries == If you want to look up URLs of former government ministries on the wayback machine, they are listed here: http://web.archive.org/web/19980125145714/http://www.jinjapan.org/jd/org/002001002.html#002001002026 - http://www.webcitation.org/6WiHjhitQ [[User:WhisperToMe|WhisperToMe]] ([[User talk:WhisperToMe|talk]]) 22:09, 1 March 2015 (UTC) {{Talk:Government of Japan/GA1}} {{Template:Did you know nominations/Government of Japan}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == A rogue article == "Before The Meiji Restoration, Japan was ruled by the government of a successive military shōgun." It looks as if a bumbling editor has inserted the word "a" in this sentence, not understanding that "shōgun" here is being used as a plural. If I interpret this correctly, the "a" ought to be removed. [[Special:Contributions/71.245.188.249|71.245.188.249]] ([[User talk:71.245.188.249|talk]]) 07:19, 21 September 2023 (UTC) :I think there are other problems with how ''shōgun'' is rendered in this article, but for the time being I've corrected this. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:10, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Could someone with knowledge correct this? == Under "History," there's an odd paratactic construction: :"Taiseihokan"（大政奉還）The return of political power to the Emperor (to the Imperial Court) in 1868 meant the resignation of Shōgun [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]], agreeing to "be the instrument for carrying out" the Emperor's orders. The capitalized "The" before "return" indicates the start of a sentence, so how does "Taiseihokan"（大政奉還) relate to the syntax of that following sentence? I don't know Japanese at all; Google Translate tells me that ''Taiseihokan'' means "system storage"? So could someone who understands the topic and the intention here be able to correct this? [[User:Cynwolfe|Cynwolfe]] ([[User talk:Cynwolfe|talk]]) 16:30, 15 January 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Inconsistent rendering of ''shōgun'' == The word ''shōgun'' is rendered as both ''shōgun'' and Shōgun (no italics). One rendering should be used consistently throughout the article. The article for [[shogun]] does not use italics, a macron above the o, or capitalisation because the word is pretty well known t English speaking audiences. I think this would be fine. But if the macron should be kept then every instance should be in italics because it's a romanisation. And there's no reason to capitalise it. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:14, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Change how ''kazoku'' is rendered == I think it may be slightly confusing for people with some knowledge of Japanese but not much knowledge of the Japanese feudal system that there is no kanji given for this word. The most common word pronounced ''kazoku'' is 家族, but the word being used in this article is 華族 and they mean different things. I suggest that this be added for clarity. [[User:Tsundokuboi|Tsundokuboi]] ([[User talk:Tsundokuboi|talk]]) 12:16, 27 January 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
